title: QA Core Spec FAIL: two generators both named 'sigma' (DUPLICATE_GENERATOR_NAME)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates DUPLICATE_GENERATOR_NAME: two generators share the name 'sigma'. The validator detects this and emits DUPLICATE_GENERATOR_NAME. Generator name uniqueness is required because names are used as keys in failure_modes_emitted lookups, preserves_invariants references, and BFS transition labels. Duplicate names make the spec ambiguous: it is undefined which generator's domain_preconditions apply when 'sigma' is called, and which failure modes it emits. The QA spec model treats generators as a named set, not a sequence — names must be unique.

generators.name: sigma
generators.action: e → e + 1 (mod m); updates d and a to preserve constraints

generators.name: sigma
generators.action: e → e - 1 (mod m); inverse of first sigma

validation_checks.details: names=[sigma, sigma]; 2 names, only 1 unique — duplicate: sigma

fail_ledger.message: generators[1].name='sigma' duplicates generators[0].name='sigma'. Generator names must be unique within a QA spec. Duplicate names create ambiguity in: (1) failure_modes_emitted lookups — it is undefined which 'sigma' emits which failure modes; (2) BFS transition labels — the search graph cannot distinguish the two generators; (3) preserves_invariants cross-references — both sigma entries claim to preserve the same invariants but have different domain semantics. Rename one generator (e.g., 'sigma_inv' for the inverse).
